title: cordyline stricta var. rigidifolia
intro: Cordyline stricta var. rigidifolia, also known as the cabbage tree or tī kōuka, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and roots are used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, which are believed to promote healing, reduce pain, and boost overall health. Traditional Maori practitioners have used the plant's extracts for centuries, and modern research is now exploring its potential benefits for cardiovascular health, antioxidant activity, and immune system support.
